issues.description: Questions are generic and lack specific passage reference - they cannot be answered without an actual text to read
While objectives align with RI.6.1, questions require a specific informational text to be meaningful and assessable
Multiple choice questions reference specific paragraphs and content that don't exist without an accompanying text passage
Question complexity appears appropriate for grade 6, but cannot be fully assessed without accompanying text
Answer key provides correct answers to non-existent questions - answers reference content not provided to students
Missing essential component: the informational text passage that questions are based on

strengths: Appropriate progression from multiple choice to extended response
Good variety of question types targeting different aspects of RI.6.1
Extended response rubrics provide helpful guidance for teachers
recommendations: Include a complete informational text passage (300-500 words appropriate for grade 6)
Revise questions to reference specific, identifiable content from the provided passage
Update answer key to reflect actual passage content
Ensure all paragraph references in questions correspond to actual paragraphs in the text
